reforms.description: The Finnish government has introduced a roadmap to attract skilled professionals, aiming to double work-based immigration and triple the number of new foreign students by 2030.
reforms.package_name: Roadmap for Education-based and Work-based Immigration 2035
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing the supply of skilled labor is expected to enhance productivity and innovation capacity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The roadmap was introduced as part of the government's strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The roadmap represents a significant effort to enhance Finland's attractiveness for skilled migrants, which is crucial for addressing labor shortages and boosting innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The Roadmap for Education-based and Work-based Immigration 2035 outlines strategies to attract skilled professionals to Finland.

reforms.description: Finland is introducing R&D credits to incentivize investment in low-emissions technologies, aiming to stimulate innovation in the green industrial transition.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing R&D in low-emissions technologies, the reform is expected to enhance innovation and productivity in the green sector, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced the introduction of R&D credits for low-emissions technologies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant policy shift aimed at fostering innovation in a critical sector for Finland's economic future, with potential long-term impacts on productivity and environmental sustainability.
reforms.source_quote: The cheapest way for Finland to achieve GHG and biodiversity targets lies in reducing emissions and increasing carbon sinks on agricultural and forestry land.

reforms.description: Finland plans to expand the Talent Boost Programme to provide more stable and permanent allocations for attracting high-skilled foreign workers, addressing current and future skill shortages.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By attracting high-skilled foreign talent, the initiative aims to fill skill gaps in the labor market, thereby enhancing productivity and innovation capacity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The expansion of the Talent Boost Programme has been officially announced.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant as it aims to create a more stable framework for attracting skilled labor, which is crucial for addressing skill shortages and enhancing innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Expand the Talent Boost Programme and turn project-based measures with positive results into more stable and permanent allocations.

reforms.description: Finland aims to support low emissions industrial processes through stronger investment incentives, including public-private partnerships and seed capital to encourage private investment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing low-emission technologies, the reform is expected to stimulate innovation in green industries, leading to sustainable economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative to support low emissions industrial processes has been officially announced.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it targets the transition to a low-carbon economy, which is essential for Finland's long-term sustainability and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: Support low emissions industrial processes through stronger investment incentives by reducing risk via strategic public-private partnerships.

reforms.description: Finland has adopted legislative amendments in line with the European Union's Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA), which aims to provide a regulatory framework for crypto assets and allow traditional financial firms to offer crypto-related services.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The regulation aims to integrate cryptocurrencies safely into the financial system, potentially enhancing innovation in financial services and attracting investment.
reforms.status_evidence: Legislative amendments were adopted in line with MiCA, with partial effectiveness in June 2024.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the regulatory landscape for cryptocurrencies, which could have far-reaching implications for innovation in the financial sector and beyond.
reforms.source_quote: Finland adopted legislative amendments in line with MiCA, which became partially effective in June 2024, with full enforcement expected by the end of 2024.

reforms.description: The Finnish government has launched a review of the operating environment and objectives for higher education and research, aiming to increase the number of PhD students and streamline postgraduate studies to meet the target of allocating 4% of GDP to R&D expenditure by 2030.
reforms.component_name: PhD Student Increase and Postgraduate Streamlining
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing the number of skilled researchers through enhanced PhD programs will contribute to innovation and productivity growth in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The Ministry of Education launched a review to boost PhD student numbers and streamline postgraduate studies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative addresses critical supply constraints in higher education and aims to enhance the research capacity of Finland, which is vital for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: In November 2024 the Ministry of Education launched a review of the operating environment and objectives for higher education and research to be completed by mid-2026.

reforms.description: The Finnish government will increase R&D funding by EUR 280 million per annum between 2025 and 2030, including EUR 255 million for higher education institutions to support 1,000 new doctoral student places from 2024 to 2027.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for R&D and doctoral training is expected to enhance human capital and innovation capacity, contributing to higher productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced the increase in R&D funding as part of a new multi-year plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in public investment in R&D, aimed at addressing skill shortages and enhancing Finland's innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: As part of a new multiyear research and development plan launched in mid-2024 the government will increase R&D funding by EUR 280 million per annum between 2025 and 2030.

reforms.description: The Act on Government R&D Funding 2023 mandates the introduction of a Multiannual R&D Funding Plan every parliamentary term, aiming to increase central government funding of R&D significantly.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform is designed to ensure sustained public investment in R&D, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Act on Government R&D Funding 2023 has been passed into law.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform establishes a systematic approach to R&D funding, ensuring long-term commitment and strategic alignment with national innovation goals.
reforms.source_quote: The Act on Government R&D Funding 2023 requires a Multiannual R&D Funding Plan to be introduced every parliamentary term.

reforms.description: From 2025, the higher education funding model will allocate a 3% weight on the number of first-time enrolments, aiming to counterbalance the output incentives that currently favor degree completions over new entrants.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging first-time enrolments can enhance the supply of skilled graduates, addressing labor market needs and fostering innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding model reform has been proposed to take effect in 2025.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant adjustment to the funding model, aiming to improve access to higher education and align it with labor market demands.
reforms.source_quote: From 2025 the HEI funding model will put a 3% weight on the number of first-time enrolments, which should help to counterbalance the output incentives.

reforms.description: The government has proposed a 'one seat rule' to require students enrolling in a qualification to forgo existing study rights at or below the level of this qualification, aiming to free up places for first-time entrants.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This rule is expected to increase the availability of places for new entrants, thereby enhancing the overall educational capacity and workforce readiness.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has proposed the 'one seat rule' to improve access to higher education.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a significant step towards optimizing higher education access and addressing the issue of blocked places due to existing study rights.
reforms.source_quote: The government has proposed a 'one seat rule' that would require a student enrolling in a qualification to forgo existing study rights at or below the level of this qualification.

reforms.description: The Ministry of Education and Culture is working with higher education institutions to expand continuous education options and micro-credentials, aiming to better meet adult learning needs and labor market demands.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Expanding continuous education and micro-credentials can enhance workforce adaptability and skill renewal, supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Ministry is working to increase short qualifications and continuous education options.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to adapt the education system to changing labor market needs and improve adult learning opportunities.
reforms.source_quote: Expanding continuing education options is a policy priority and the Ministry of Education and Culture is working with HEIs to increase these short qualifications.

reforms.description: Finland's funding model for higher education institutions has been reformed to incentivize on-time completion of degrees, with a funding coefficient that rewards institutions based on student completion rates.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing timely degree completion, the reform aims to enhance the skills supply in the labor market, addressing skill shortages and improving productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding model has been adjusted to include completion incentives.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the funding model for higher education, aiming to improve educational outcomes and labor market alignment.
reforms.source_quote: Finland’s funding model has been reformed and now also incentivises on-time completion with a funding coefficient of 1.8 for on-time completion.

reforms.description: The government has introduced initiatives to attract and retain high-skilled foreign workers, including the Talent Boost Programme and streamlined residence permit processes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Attracting skilled immigrants enhances the labor force's capabilities, driving innovation and productivity growth in key sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: Government initiatives such as the Talent Boost Programme have been launched to enhance the recruitment of skilled talent.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant for addressing skill shortages and enhancing the innovation capacity of the economy, though it builds on existing frameworks.
reforms.source_quote: Government initiatives, such as the Talent Boost Programme and streamlined residence permit processes, provide a solid foundation for recruiting skilled talent.

reforms.description: Finland has implemented a two-year post-graduation job-seeking permit for non-EU/EEA foreign graduates, allowing them to remain in the country and search for employment after completing their studies.
reforms.package_name: Roadmap for Education-based and Work-based Immigration 2035
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform enhances the retention of skilled graduates, contributing to the labor market and innovation capacity in Finland.
reforms.status_evidence: The job-seeking permit has been put into effect since 2022.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses the integration of foreign graduates into the workforce, which is crucial for maintaining a skilled labor pool in Finland.
reforms.source_quote: Finland has implemented a two-year post-graduation job-seeking permit for non-EU/EEA foreign graduates in 2022.

reforms.description: The Finnish government has reduced the tax rate for high-skilled foreign workers and researchers to 32% for up to seven years, extending the maximum duration of the tax scheme by three years starting from 2024.
reforms.component_name: Tax relief for high-skilled foreign workers
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This tax relief aims to attract and retain high-skilled talent, enhancing the innovation capacity of the Finnish economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced the extension of the tax scheme in 2023.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant in enhancing Finland's attractiveness to foreign talent, which is essential for innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government reduced the tax rate by 3 percentage points in 2020 and extended the maximum duration of the tax scheme by three years starting from 2024.

reforms.description: The Finnish government has introduced a comprehensive, multiannual plan to guide the use of central government R&D funding, focusing on strengthening cooperation between businesses, higher education institutions, and research organisations, with an additional EUR 1 billion in public investments.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increases public investment in R&D, fostering collaboration and innovation, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan has been adopted and is in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in public R&D funding and a strategic shift towards enhancing collaboration between sectors, which is crucial for long-term innovation and economic competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The government has adopted a comprehensive, multiannual plan in 2024 to guide the use of central government R&D funding.

reforms.description: Finland has introduced a fixed-term tax credit to encourage private sector investment in large-scale green tech projects, covering up to 20% of eligible investment costs, aimed at accelerating the clean transition.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages private investment in green technologies, which can lead to innovation and economic growth while addressing environmental challenges.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit is expected to be implemented in 2025.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant step towards incentivizing private sector investment in green technologies, which is essential for Finland's transition to a low-carbon economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government has introduced a fixed-term tax credit to encourage the private sector to carry out large-scale green tech projects.

reforms.description: The Finnish government has reaffirmed its support for expanding grid capacity and enhancing infrastructure, allocating approximately EUR 2.22 billion in the 2025 Budget to promote carbon neutrality and the green transition, which includes EUR 94 million specifically for renewable energy and energy efficiency projects.
reforms.package_name: 2025 Renewable Energy and Energy Efficiency Package
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investments in grid infrastructure are essential for supporting renewable energy deployment, which enhances energy security and promotes industrial growth in energy-intensive sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The government support for expanding grid capacity has been reaffirmed in the Government Programme.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in infrastructure critical for the transition to a low-carbon economy, impacting multiple sectors and ensuring long-term energy security.
reforms.source_quote: The 2025 Budget proposal allocating approximately EUR 2.22 billion to promote carbon neutrality and the green transition.

reforms.description: Fingrid has outlined extensive plans to enhance grid capacity through investments amounting to approximately EUR 4 billion over the next decade, focusing on strengthening internal transmission capacity and cross-border connections.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing grid capacity is vital for accommodating increased renewable energy production and ensuring stability in energy supply, which supports industrial growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Fingrid has outlined extensive plans to enhance grid capacity through investments.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a major infrastructure initiative that will significantly impact Finland's energy landscape and support the transition to a low-carbon economy.
reforms.source_quote: Fingrid has outlined extensive plans to enhance grid capacity through investments amounting to approximately EUR 4 billion over the next decade.

reforms.description: Finland has introduced a tax credit to incentivise the development of hydrogen infrastructure, aiming to lower barriers for private investment in renewable hydrogen production facilities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credit reduces investment risks and encourages private sector participation in the development of hydrogen technologies, which are crucial for decarbonising industries.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has introduced a tax credit to support hydrogen infrastructure development.
reforms.importance_rationale: The tax credit is a significant step towards enhancing private investment in hydrogen infrastructure, which is essential for Finland's green transition and industrial competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The Finnish government has introduced a tax credit to incentivise the development of hydrogen infrastructure such as production facilities.

reforms.description: The Finnish government is implementing a new decree to mandate climate reports and lists of construction products for new buildings, aimed at harmonising low-carbon assessments and ensuring compliance with forthcoming limit values during the final inspection phase.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform is expected to enhance the sustainability of the construction sector, promoting innovation in low-carbon building practices and materials.
reforms.status_evidence: The decree is expected to come into force on January 1, 2026.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant step towards enhancing environmental standards in construction, with potential long-term impacts on the sector's sustainability and innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry of the Environment has announced a new decree to mandate climate reports and lists of construction products for new buildings.

reforms.description: The Finnish government is enhancing its efforts to attract and retain high-skilled foreign workers in green sectors by streamlining immigration processes and providing support for professional development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By attracting skilled foreign workers, Finland aims to fill critical skill gaps in emerging green industries, thereby enhancing innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has introduced a range of measures aimed at streamlining immigration processes for highly skilled workers.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant for addressing skill shortages in green sectors, which are crucial for Finland's transition to a sustainable economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government has introduced a range of measures aimed at streamlining immigration processes for highly skilled workers.

reforms.description: Finland plans to enhance its support for venture capital investment in green start-ups and SMEs by establishing dedicated green venture capital funds or offering tax incentives to venture capitalists focused on clean technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to venture capital, the initiative aims to stimulate innovation and growth in the green technology sector, which is crucial for Finland's transition to a low-emission economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced plans to establish dedicated green venture capital funds.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding strategy aimed at fostering innovation in the green sector, which is vital for Finland's economic and environmental goals.
reforms.source_quote: Finland could enhance its support for venture capital investment in green start-ups and SMEs by establishing dedicated green venture capital funds or offering tax incentives to venture capitalists focused on clean technologies.

reforms.description: The Finnish government granted EUR 72.6 million in investment aid to 13 clean energy projects in 2024, including significant funding for renewable hydrogen projects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This funding is expected to stimulate innovation in clean energy technologies and create jobs, contributing to Finland's green transition.
reforms.status_evidence: The government granted EUR 72.6 million in investment aid to clean energy projects.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding initiative is a significant investment in clean energy, which is crucial for Finland's environmental goals and economic development.
reforms.source_quote: The government granted EUR 72.6 million in investment aid to 13 clean energy projects in 2024.

reforms.description: Finland introduced a voluntary biodiversity offset system as part of the Nature Conservation Act 2023, aimed at compensating for biodiversity loss due to development projects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The offset system is designed to encourage private investment in biodiversity, aligning economic development with conservation efforts.
reforms.status_evidence: The biodiversity offset system was introduced as part of the Nature Conservation Act.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a biodiversity offset system represents a significant policy shift towards integrating economic activities with environmental conservation, potentially mobilizing private investment.
reforms.source_quote: Finland introduced a voluntary biodiversity offset system in the Nature Conservation Act 2023.
